movements.heroDescription: Explore clearly structured movements: from classical currents to contemporary practices. We highlight key ideas, notable artists and typical media so you can quickly grasp each movement's character and influence.
movements.noResults: No movements found for the selected criteria. Try clearing filters or changing the search phrase.

renaissance.title: Renaissance
renaissance.summary: Revival of the human spirit and science, returning to classical ideals, perspective and harmony. Art becomes rational and anatomically precise, aiming for balance, clear composition and controlled light.

details.overview: Renaissance art is grounded in humanism and classical models, striving for accurate depiction of the human body and the world through observation and proportion. The artist is seen as both creator and scholar, combining art with scientific inquiry. Individual identity gains importance through portraiture and new subject matter.
details.implementation: Ideas were realized through linear perspective, anatomical study, chiaroscuro and oil painting; compositions are balanced, clear and often driven by patronage. Geometry and mathematical ratios guide spatial construction, while light is controlled to model form. Frescoes, altarpieces and architectural orders reinforce a sense of harmony and classical order.

baroque.title: Baroque
baroque.summary: Drama, movement and emotion. Theatrical contrasts, deep shadows and monumental scale create a powerful spectacle. Dynamic compositions emphasize pathos, directional light and a strong impact on the viewer.

details.overview: Baroque art seeks emotional impact and immersive spectacle, emphasizing movement, drama and narrative intensity. It is designed to persuade and overwhelm, drawing the viewer into the scene through theatrical staging. The boundary between artwork and real space is often blurred.
details.implementation: Strong light contrasts, diagonal compositions, energetic brushwork, monumental forms and theatrical lighting amplify the viewer's experience. Figures are shown in motion, expressions are heightened, and space expands with illusionistic effects. Architecture embraces curves, ornament and optical tricks to intensify the experience.

artists.name: Rembrandt
works: The Anatomy Lesson of Dr. Nicolaes Tulp

romanticism.title: Romanticism
romanticism.summary: Individual emotions, the sublime in nature and ideas of freedom. Art becomes a language of feeling, with mood, drama and personal experience at the center. Landscapes often convey nature as an awe-inspiring force.

details.overview: Romanticism emphasizes subjectivity, freedom, the grandeur of nature and historical narratives as vehicles for inner experience. Emotion and imagination outweigh reason, and art becomes a record of personal and spiritual states. Folklore, history and the exotic help build a sense of awe and mystery.
details.implementation: Ideas are realized through dramatic landscapes, intensified color, liberated composition, striking light effects and symbolic motifs. Painters employ contrast, stormy skies and dynamic brushwork to heighten emotion. Figures are often isolated within vast nature to stress the human encounter with the sublime.

artists.name: Caspar David Friedrich
works: Wanderer above the Sea of Fog

impressionism.title: Impressionism
impressionism.summary: Everyday moments and shifting light. Fast brushwork and an open palette capture fleeting impressions. Much is painted outdoors to seize atmosphere, movement and shimmering color.

details.overview: Impressionists aimed to capture transient light and atmosphere, depicting modern life without academic rigidity. The momentary sensation matters more than precise detail, and everyday scenes gain poetic value. The movement freed painting from historical subjects and opened it to contemporary experience.
details.implementation: They painted outdoors with quick strokes, transparent palettes, attention to colored shadows and a sense of motion. Colors are placed side by side to mix optically, and contours remain soft. Compositions feel like snapshots, emphasizing immediacy and movement.

artists.name: Pierre-Auguste Renoir
works: Dance at Le Moulin de la Galette

expressionism.title: Expressionism
expressionism.summary: Bold colors and distorted form intensify emotional impact. Inner states, tension and anxiety matter more than realistic depiction. Compositions are often jagged and highly charged.

details.overview: Expressionism stresses inner emotion and psychological tension, deliberately moving away from realistic depiction. Art becomes a language of feeling, expressing anxiety, joy, fear or existential pressure. What is felt matters more than what is seen.
details.implementation: Forms are distorted, colors intensified, contours sharpened and compositions destabilized to heighten emotional charge. Brushwork is raw, space is compressed or warped, and symbolism helps translate inner experience into visual form.

cubism.title: Cubism
cubism.summary: Breaking objects into geometric planes and combining multiple viewpoints. The image is analyzed, fragmented and reassembled into a new structure. Emphasis falls on form, construction and spatial perception.

details.overview: Cubism investigates object structure by breaking it into geometric planes and merging multiple viewpoints. It rejects single-point perspective, proposing a more analytical view of objects and space. The result is a rational, structural approach to form.
details.implementation: Fragmentation, collage, restrained palettes, strong construction and overlapping forms define the visual language. Analytic cubism favors muted tones and complex planes, while synthetic cubism introduces brighter color, typography and pasted materials. Objects dissolve into structure without losing coherence.

surrealism.title: Surrealism
surrealism.summary: The unconscious, dreams and association. Breaking logic with unexpected imagery creates paradoxical worlds. Automatism, free association and psychoanalytic themes are central.

details.overview: Surrealism aims to reveal the unconscious and dream logic by blending reality with fantasy. Influenced by psychoanalysis, it explores desire, fear and symbolic meaning. Art becomes a field where logic is suspended and imagination rules.
details.implementation: Paradoxical imagery, automatism, free association and unexpected object juxtapositions create uncanny narratives. Some artists paint dream scenes with hyperreal clarity; others rely on spontaneous drawing and collage. The result is a disorienting but poetic visual language.

artists.name: Leonora Carrington
works: The Inn of the Dawn Horse

abstract-expressionism.title: Abstract Expressionism
abstract-expressionism.summary: Large-scale gestures and spontaneous action reveal inner tension. The canvas becomes an arena for rhythm, movement and process. Recognizable objects often dissolve into pure abstraction.

details.overview: Abstract Expressionism foregrounds the act of painting itself, with gesture and scale as primary content. The canvas becomes an arena for action, and painting is treated as an event. Artists seek to convey raw energy, inner tension or meditative stillness.
details.implementation: Spontaneous brushwork, drip techniques, large formats, color fields and rhythmic movement define the approach. Action painting emphasizes gesture and motion, while color-field painting relies on expansive, quiet color. Figurative references fade to prioritize emotional experience.

pop-art.title: Pop Art
pop-art.summary: Mass culture, advertising and everyday objects. Irony, bright colors and repetition expose symbols of consumer society. Art adopts the language of popular culture.

details.overview: Pop Art reflects mass culture and consumption, translating everyday imagery into fine art. It collapses the boundary between high art and popular media, highlighting the power of brands, celebrities and advertising. The work often reads as both celebration and critique.
details.implementation: Advertising and comic aesthetics, bright colors, repetition, serial production and irony dominate the visual language. Screen printing, photographic reproduction and bold outlines are common. Repetition amplifies the logic of mass production and consumer desire.

artists.name: Richard Hamilton
works: Just what is it that makes today's homes so different, so appealing?

minimalism.title: Minimalism
minimalism.summary: Less is more. Simple forms, clear structure and material presence. Emotional expression is minimized while proportion, rhythm and spatial experience take priority.

details.overview: Minimalism seeks maximum reduction and clear structure, emphasizing the object and its relationship to space. Emotional narrative is minimized, while material presence, scale and perception become central. It reacts against expressive excess with a cool, rational aesthetic.
details.implementation: Geometric, repetitive forms avoid decoration; material qualities, proportions and viewer experience are central. Modular systems, serial repetition and industrial materials are common. The object is presented as a literal thing in space rather than a representation.

contemporary.title: Contemporary Art
contemporary.summary: A plural, global scene: technology, identity, social themes and new formats. Art often includes installations, performance and interdisciplinary practices. Concept, context and social dialogue are central.

details.overview: Contemporary art spans diverse practices, addressing identity, politics and technology within a global context. It is plural and open-ended, prioritizing concept, context and social dialogue. Art often engages with current events and community participation while crossing traditional genres.
details.implementation: Installations, performance, interactive formats, documentary strategies and cross-disciplinary projects are common. Technology, archives, social media and public space become part of the work. The artwork may be a process, experience or discussion rather than a static object.
